message.content:
Create the feedback triage workflow documentation for the Kapow project. This documents how Claude Code can triage user feedback from GitHub Issues and promote actionable items to beads.

## 1. Create `docs/specs/feedback-triage.md`

Write a clear, actionable spec document:

```markdown
# Feedback Triage Workflow

User feedback submitted through the Kapow feedback form is synced to GitHub
Issues in the `ketang/kapow` repository. This document describes the triage
workflow for analyzing and acting on feedback.

## Labels

### Feedback source labels (auto-applied by sync tool)
- `feedback:bug` — User-reported bug
- `feedback:feature` — Feature request
- `feedback:general` — General feedback or question

### Triage labels (applied during triage)
- `priority:high` — Impacts core functionality or many users
- `priority:low` — Minor issue or nice-to-have
- `triage:duplicate` — Duplicate of existing issue
- `triage:wontfix` — Not actionable or out of scope
- `triage:needs-info` — Needs more information to act on

## Triage process

### 1. List untriaged feedback

```bash
gh issue list --label "feedback:bug" --label "feedback:feature" --label "feedback:general" --state open --json number,title,labels,createdAt
```

Or filter by specific category:
```bash
gh issue list --label "feedback:bug" --state open
```

### 2. Analyze each issue

For each open feedback issue:
1. Read the issue body: `gh issue view <number>`
2. Assess severity and actionability
3. Check for duplicates against existing beads issues and GitHub issues

### 3. Apply triage labels

```bash
gh issue edit <number> --add-label "priority:high"
```

### 4. Promote actionable items to beads

For feedback that warrants development work, create a beads issue:

```bash
bd create --title "Fix: <summary from feedback>" \
  --type bug \
  --description "From user feedback: https://github.com/ketang/kapow/issues/<number>

<description from feedback>" \
  --external-ref "gh-<number>" \
  --priority 2
```

Use `--type bug` for bugs, `--type feature` for feature requests.

The `--external-ref` flag links the beads issue back to the GitHub issue for
traceability.

### 5. Close non-actionable issues

For duplicates:
```bash
gh issue close <number> --comment "Duplicate of #<other> — closing."
```

For won't-fix:
```bash
gh issue close <number> --comment "Thank you for the feedback. After review, this is out of scope for the current roadmap."
```

For needs-info (don't close, just label):
```bash
gh issue edit <number> --add-label "triage:needs-info"
gh issue comment <number> --body "Thanks for reporting! Could you provide more details about..."
```

## Periodic triage

Run triage periodically (e.g., weekly or after a batch of feedback syncs).
Ask Claude: "Triage open feedback issues" to kick off the process.

## Feedback sync tool

The sync tool lives at `tools/feedbacksync/`. To sync pending feedback:

```bash
cd tools/feedbacksync
go run . --database-url "$DATABASE_URL" --github-token "$GITHUB_TOKEN"
```

Use `--dry-run` to preview without creating issues.
```

## 2. Update `CLAUDE.md` (project root)

Add a new recipe to the "Common task recipes" section in `/home/ketan/project/kapow/CLAUDE.md`. Add it after the "Add a database query" recipe:

```markdown
### Triage user feedback

See [docs/specs/feedback-triage.md](docs/specs/feedback-triage.md) for the
full workflow. Quick reference:

1. Sync pending feedback: `cd tools/feedbacksync && go run . --database-url "$DATABASE_URL" --github-token "$GITHUB_TOKEN"`
2. List untriaged: `gh issue list --label "feedback:bug" --label "feedback:feature" --label "feedback:general" --state open`
3. Promote to beads: `bd create --title "Fix: ..." --type bug --external-ref "gh-<number>"`
```

Working directory is `/home/ketan/project/kapow`. Use absolute paths for all file operations.

content.text: I'll create the feedback triage spec and update the project CLAUDE.md.

content.content:
80→| Standard | `make test-standard` | ~30-60s | None | Before committing |
    81→| Full | `make test-full` | ~2-5min | DB + running services | Before merge |
    82→
    83→### Core rules
    84→
    85→- **Always write tests for new additions.** Tests ship with the code, not as a follow-up.
    86→- **Update existing tests when modifying behaviour.**
    87→- **Target ≥ 80% line coverage per package.** Glue/wiring packages (`cmd/server`, `router`) are exempt.
    88→- **Always run database code against a real database.** Compilation and unit tests alone are not sufficient — runtime issues like pgx type encoding mismatches only surface with a live connection.
    89→
    90→---
    91→
    92→## Issue tracking (Beads)
    93→
    94→```bash
    95→bd list                                # all open issues
    96→bd ready                               # issues with no blockers (ready to work)
    97→bd show <id>                           # detailed view with dependencies
    98→bd create --title "..." --description "..."
    99→bd update <id> --status in_progress
   100→bd close <id>
   101→```
   102→
   103→When converting approved plans into beads issues, prefer **thin end-to-end
   104→slices** that deliver a complete user or agent outcome. Do not split one
   105→behaviour into separate database, API, and frontend issues unless the work is a
   106→true standalone prerequisite.
   107→
   108→See `AGENTS.md` for issue type guidance, epic creation, dependencies, and
   109→completion workflow. See `AGENTS.md` for the detailed issue-sizing rules,

agent-workflow.md
authentication.md
ci-design.md
contract-gates.md
contract-lifecycle.md
contract-traceability.md
data-model.md
deployment.md
e2e-ci-design.md
frontend-architecture.md
generated
graphql-api.md
product-overview.md
search-engine.md

122:### Add a database query
123-
124-Use pgx directly: `pool.QueryRow(ctx, sql, $1, $2, ...)`. Always pass context.
125-Never build SQL with `fmt.Sprintf` — always use `$N` placeholders.
126-
127-### Add a Go package
128-
129-Create `api/internal/<name>/` with `package <name>`. Pass deps explicitly via
130-structs; no `init()` globals.
131-
132----

content.text:
Done. Two changes made:

1. **Created** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/docs/specs/feedback-triage.md` -- the full feedback triage workflow spec covering labels, the 5-step triage process, periodic triage guidance, and the feedbacksync tool reference.

2. **Updated** `/home/ketan/project/kapow/CLAUDE.md` -- added a "Triage user feedback" recipe in the "Common task recipes" section, right after "Add a database query" and before "Add a Go package".
